title: Thinking and Intelligence
learningObjectives: Describe the nature of concepts, prototypes, and schemas in cognitive processing
Explain the relationship between language and thought, including key theories of language development
Compare problem-solving strategies such as algorithms, heuristics, and trial and error
Distinguish between different theories of intelligence and the concept of creativity
Evaluate the major measures of intelligence, including their reliability and validity
Analyze the roles of genetics and environment in shaping intelligence
